title: Professorens bedste investering: Sådan tjente han stort på Danske Bank
summary_md:
# Professorens bedste investering: Sådan tjente han stort på Danske Bank

**Feed**: borsen-podcast  
**Published**: 2026-06-01  
**Market Sentiment**: Mixed

⏱️ **Time Horizon Key**: ST = <3 months | MT = 3-12 months | LT = >12 months

---

## Episode Thesis

A personal-finance interview with professor Kent Backman about his low-risk, highly diversified investing philosophy rather than a market-outlook episode. He favours low-cost index funds/investment associations (he chairs Great Dane Investors), holds almost no single stocks, and recounts his best trade — buying Danske Bank during the 2008 financial crisis and selling at a multiple years later. He warns against single-stock tips, hype-driven megatrends (defense, AI/tech), and speculative bets like Bitcoin.

---

## Universe Stocks

### ⛔ BTC — Bitcoin | **AVOID** (Moderate)

**Sentiment**: Negative | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 60%

Warns retail investors against betting on Bitcoin/crypto for quick gains given extreme volatility and survivorship bias in success stories.

#### Reasoning

Backman respects the technology but cautions that believing a couple of clicks secures a million ignores symmetric downside — for every big winner someone loses correspondingly when Bitcoin falls. He frames it as taking on outsized risk a single individual cannot afford, contradicting his diversified, low-risk philosophy.

#### Sell Triggers

- if investor can genuinely afford to lose the stake
- if held as a tiny diversified allocation rather than a get-rich bet

> *"att bara tro att man med ett par enkla klick så är man säkert en miljon eller mer vid att investera i bitcoin alltså att löpa så stor risiko"*

---

### 🟡 MAERSK-B — A.P. Møller-Mærsk | **HOLD** (Weak)

**Sentiment**: Positive | **Time Horizon**: LT  
**Confidence**: 50%

His only single stock, held long-term for sentimental/value reasons; says he keeps it given reasonable long-run performance and dividends.

#### Reasoning

Backman bought Maersk after teaching its employees, partly philanthropic and partly because he admires the company's proud traditions. He notes it has moved up and down but performed sensibly on a long horizon with decent dividends, so he simply keeps it rather than treating it as an active position.

#### Sell Triggers

- if he needs liquidity for a major purchase
- if the long-term performance/dividend case deteriorates

> *"så har jag simpelthen valt att behålla den"*

---
